subject: Ecology
title: Sharks as Apex Regulators
description: A 60-second lesson on Sharks as Apex Regulators.

hook.text: Sharks have patrolled the oceans for 450 million years—longer than trees have existed.

buildup.text: As apex predators, they keep prey populations healthy by removing sick and weak individuals.

discovery.text: When shark numbers drop, mid-level predators boom and overeat their own prey.

twist.text: Humans kill roughly 100 million sharks per year, mostly for fins. Sharks kill about five humans.

climax.text: Losing sharks destabilizes food webs that took hundreds of millions of years to form.

punchline.text: Sharks aren't the threat. Their absence is.

quiz.question: What happens when shark populations decline?
options: Mid-level predators boom and overeat their prey
